title: Use streams for pure transformations, not side effects
description: Express data transformations as side-effect-free stream pipelines with collectors, and fall back to loops when mutation or early exit fits better.
body: Use when transforming or aggregating collections in Java. Do not mutate external state inside map/forEach, run a parallelStream over a non-thread-safe accumulator, or chain streams where a plain loop reads clearer. Actions: keep lambdas pure, terminate with collectors (toList, groupingBy, toMap with a merge function), use the three-arg reduce only when associative, and reserve parallelStream for large CPU-bound, stateless workloads measured to benefit. Validation signals include identical results between sequential and parallel runs and no ConcurrentModificationException. Risk boundary: a side-effecting parallel stream produces nondeterministic or corrupted results; if ordering or shared mutation matters, deny parallelism and use an explicit loop or a thread-safe collector.
